title: [Comb] Avoid infinite loops by not canonicalizing recursive ops

features.name: self_referential_comb_add
features.description: Code should include a comb.add operation where the result variable is used as its own operand, creating a self-referential cyclic dependency. This recursive pattern cannot be safely folded or canonicalized without causing infinite loops in the CIRCT canonicalizer.

features.name: recursive_comb_and_prevents_folding
features.description: Code should include a comb.and (or similar binary combinational op) that references its own output as an input operand, forming a trivial self-recursion. The CIRCT canonicalizer must detect such trivially recursive ops and skip folding to avoid infinite loops.

features.name: loopy_combinational_dataflow_process
features.description: Code should include SystemVerilog combinational process or dataflow logic where a value is computed and fed back to itself through combinational operations. Such loopy dataflow describes cyclic computations that cannot be reduced by canonicalization.
